Title: Flux Product Hero That Sells: Studio E-Commerce Shot With Crisp, Correctly-Spelled Label
Teaser: Writes one natural-language FLUX.1 prompt for a conversion-ready studio product hero with true material texture and a perfectly legible on-pack label.
PromptHint: Name the [PRODUCT] and its material; set the exact [BRAND/LABEL TEXT] and an [ASPECT RATIO].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a commercial product photographer who writes natural-language prompts for FLUX.1 (which reads flowing sentences, not Midjourney comma-keyword stacks, and renders quoted text reliably). GOAL: Turn my brief into ONE photoreal, listing-ready e-commerce hero image. INPUTS — Product: [PRODUCT]. Exact label text to render: [BRAND/LABEL TEXT]. Aspect ratio: [ASPECT RATIO]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not show this): identify the product's real material and finish, decide where the key light must sit to reveal that texture, and how to keep the label front-facing and unwarped. THEN write ONE flowing descriptive paragraph that names: the product with its exact material and finish; a clean seamless studio sweep with a soft gradient; a three-point lighting setup (large softbox key at 45 degrees, subtle rim/separation light, gentle fill) with one controlled specular highlight; a shallow-depth-of-field 100mm macro look; true-to-life color, accurate reflections and realistic micro-texture; and the label reading the exact words "[BRAND/LABEL TEXT]" in a clean sans-serif, sharp and correctly spelled. CONSTRAINTS: photoreal only; no extra props; no warped, doubled, or gibberish text; physically plausible reflections. Express the aspect ratio in plain words inside the paragraph (e.g. "square 1:1 composition", "vertical 4:5 frame") — FLUX.1 does not use --ar flags. OUTPUT FORMAT (exactly, nothing else): 1) PROMPT: <one copy-paste paragraph, ending with the stated aspect ratio in words> 2) TEXT TIP: <one line on keeping "[BRAND/LABEL TEXT]" legible in FLUX, e.g. shorten or quote it>

Title: Flux Storefront Mockup: Photoreal Shop Window With Your Exact Sign Wording
Teaser: Generates one FLUX.1 prompt for a believable street-level storefront where your real business name renders sharp and correctly spelled on the signage.
PromptHint: Give the [BUSINESS TYPE], the exact [SIGN TEXT], and an optional [CITY/STREET VIBE].
Prompt: ROLE: You are an architectural and signage photographer writing for FLUX.1, which renders legible text best when you quote it and keep it short. GOAL: Create ONE photoreal, street-level storefront image. INPUTS — Business: [BUSINESS TYPE]. Exact sign wording: [SIGN TEXT]. Setting vibe: [CITY/STREET VIBE]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not show): pick the time of day and weather that flatter this shopfront, and place the sign so every word stays readable head-on. THEN write ONE descriptive paragraph naming: the shopfront and its real materials (brick, glass, painted wood, metal trim); a fascia or hanging sign reading exactly "[SIGN TEXT]" in a fitting typeface at high contrast; the lighting (golden-hour side light or warm evening window glow); realistic reflections in the glass; a wide 24mm street perspective; a clean, people-free foreground; fine surface texture and natural weathering; shot on a full-frame camera at f/4. CONSTRAINTS: spelling exact; no duplicated, floating, or melted letters; no cartoon look; no crowds. State the aspect ratio in words in the sentence — FLUX.1 uses no --ar flag. OUTPUT FORMAT (exactly, nothing else): 1) PROMPT: <one copy-paste paragraph, ending "in a wide 3:2 landscape frame"> 2) SIGN FIX: <one line on what to do if a letter renders wrong> 3) NIGHT VARIANT: <one sentence swapping the scene to neon-lit dusk>

Title: Flux Editorial Portrait: Magazine-Cover Lighting With Real Skin and Named Lens

INPUTS — Subject: [SUBJECT]. Mood / story: [MOOD/STORY]. Wardrobe and color: [WARDROBE/COLOR]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not show): name the single emotion the portrait must carry, then choose the lighting that earns it. THEN write ONE rich descriptive paragraph naming: the subject and exact expression; a clear setting or seamless backdrop; an explicit lighting plan (e.g. soft beauty-dish key, subtle hair light, deep shadow falloff for drama); realistic skin texture with visible pores and natural imperfections (no plastic over-smoothing); the stated wardrobe and color palette; a named lens and aperture (85mm at f/1.4 for compression and creamy bokeh); eye-level framing; a neutral-to-cinematic color grade; shot on a full-frame mirrorless body.
